description: Declarative mode registry: the single source of truth for the discriminator (workflowMode/packetKind/backendKind) PLUS the advisorRouting projection for sk-design. The hub reads this file to resolve a mode; the advisor routes any design query to the single identity sk-design and the hub picks the mode. All six modes route by hub membership (routingClass 'metadata'), so there is no advisor map entry per mode and no merged-identity projection — the advisor does not read this file at runtime.

discriminator.workflowMode: Public hub/mode key. Stable identity used by the hub router and this registry. Carried by every mode.
discriminator.packetKind: The axis discriminator: 'workflow' for the five design-judgment modes (interface/foundations/motion/audit/md-generator) that act on a design axis; 'transport' for a packet that bridges to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface rather than performing design judgment itself.
discriminator.backendKind: Which backend runs the mode: reference-base (cites the shared design reference base under references/), playwright-extract (runs the embedded Playwright CSS-extraction pipeline), or od-cli-transport (drives the external Open Design app's od CLI and stdio MCP server).

advisorRoutingContract.routingClass: How the advisor finds the mode. For sk-design every mode is 'metadata' = resolved by hub membership; the advisor routes the single identity sk-design and the hub picks the mode, so no mode carries an advisor map entry or a merged-identity projection.
advisorRoutingContract.packetSkillName: The packet's SKILL.md name. For sk-design the packet folder IS its packetSkillName — the prefixed name (e.g. folder 'design-interface' == packetSkillName 'design-interface') — so no mode has a folder/name mismatch and grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false throughout.
advisorRoutingContract.grandfatheredFolderMismatch: true only when a packet folder differs from its packetSkillName (a preserved legacy mismatch). All six sk-design modes use folder == packetSkillName (the prefixed name), so none has a mismatch — grandfatheredFolderMismatch is false for all six.
advisorRoutingContract.command: The bound slash command for the mode, recorded even on metadata-routed modes (same field sk-doc's mode-registry.json carries); null when the mode has no dedicated command today. The five design-judgment modes each have a dedicated /design:* command; the sixth mode, the design-mcp-open-design transport, carries command:null and is reached only by mandatory pairing with a design-judgment mode, never by its own command.

transform-verbs.description: Declares the transform-verb routing extension: framing-split verb aliases (interface-frame 'make it' applies the move; audit-frame 'should it be' evaluates it), with excludedAliases and aliasOnly carve-outs. Activates the in-place top-level transformVerbRouting block without relocating it — the hub router vocabulary, command projection parity, and playbook scenarios all read it at that location.

transport-axis.description: Declares the TRANSPORT axis. packetKind:'transport' entries bridge to an external tool's CLI/MCP surface (backendKind:'od-cli-transport', routingClass:'metadata', mutatesWorkspace:false since writes land in the external tool, not this repo). They never perform design judgment themselves — every entry on this axis has its own mandatory-pairing contract requiring sk-design's own workflow modes before any design-affecting operation.

transformVerbRouting.note: Transform verbs split by framing: audit asks whether the move should happen; interface applies the requested move. excludedAliases governs free-text transform-verb framing only (e.g. 'make it typeset'); command-metadata.json's taskProjections is a separate layer governing which mode owns the SAME verb under an explicit slash command (e.g. /design:foundations typeset). The two intentionally name the same verbs for different resolution paths -- this is not a collision.

aliases: drive open design from the terminal
